why_include: Core pathway in the StrokeCog paper. Immune proteins were a large fraction of SIS3-correlated proteins, most immune proteins were higher with worse mood, and the authors propose aberrant peripheral immune activation as a mechanism.
literature_basis: Bidoki et al. 2023 StrokeCog SIS3 paper; post-stroke depression proteomics; ischemic stroke inflammation outcome studies.

why_include: Prior post-stroke depression proteomic studies reported complement and coagulation cascade dysregulation; stroke proteomics studies repeatedly identify coagulation and inflammatory proteins.
literature_basis: Nguyen et al. 2016 Frontiers Neurology; ischemic stroke proteomics studies.

why_include: The StrokeCog paper reported that cell death and stress response pathways were markedly elevated as mood worsened. Oxidative stress is also a reported mechanism in post-stroke cognitive impairment and depression-related biology.
literature_basis: Bidoki et al. 2023; PSCI omics review; MDD pathway reviews.

why_include: The StrokeCog paper highlights mTOR pathway changes and NF-kB/inflammatory signaling as mechanistically relevant to chronic post-stroke mood.
literature_basis: Bidoki et al. 2023; ischemic stroke outcome inflammation Olink study.

why_include: The StrokeCog paper explicitly interprets QDPR and KYNU as evidence for altered tryptophan/serotonin pathway activity in worse mood.
literature_basis: Bidoki et al. 2023; depression tryptophan metabolism literature.

why_include: The StrokeCog paper proposes immune activation leading to reduced serotonin activity and altered neuronal plasticity; MDD literature converges on synaptic/neuroplasticity pathways.
literature_basis: Bidoki et al. 2023; major depression molecular pathway reviews.

literature_aligned_pathway: Integrin / ECM / cell adhesion / vascular remodeling
why_include: The StrokeCog paper treated integrins as a distinct functional group and noted that integrin-associated proteins were unique in directionality. ECM/cell adhesion also captures vascular remodeling and neurovascular repair biology.
literature_basis: Bidoki et al. 2023; stroke vascular remodeling biology.

why_include: Stroke and cognitive impairment literature emphasizes vascular/endothelial dysfunction and BBB/neurovascular unit injury. This category is biologically important even if not a major explicit group in the StrokeCog paper.
PSCI reviews identify atherosclerosis and metabolism as mechanisms; depression literature also includes mitochondrial and metabolic dysfunction.
literature_basis: PSCI omics review; major depression pathway review.

literature_aligned_pathway: Vesicle secretion / extracellular vesicle / membrane trafficking
why_include: This is less central in the StrokeCog paper but may capture a PID39-like subtype and is relevant to extracellular-vesicle/exosome proteomics in post-stroke cognitive biology.
literature_basis: Exploratory subtype result; post-stroke cognitive impairment exosome literature.

why_include: Depression biology often includes stress/neuroendocrine systems, but this category is broad and needs biologist review in this plasma-protein context.
literature_basis: MDD pathway review; PSD mechanism reviews.

literature_aligned_pathway: Systemic organ injury / leakage / comorbidity markers
why_include: Useful as a systemic health/comorbidity signal but should not be interpreted as a specific neuro-mood mechanism without review.
literature_basis: Clinical interpretation category; not a primary paper mechanism.
